summary: O'Neil reports on a theory circulating among her contacts that Larry Summers is being positioned as a Fed Chair candidate alongside Janet Yellen purely as a political maneuver. The theory suggests Obama would ultimately choose Yellen, allowing him to appear progressive on gender while also signaling he's learned from past mistakes in handling the financial crisis. O'Neil finds the theory plausible depending on her mood but remains worried about the outcome.
key_points: Larry Summers may be a deliberate 'straw man' candidate for Fed Chair to make Yellen's selection look better
The political calculus would let Obama appear as a champion for women while distancing himself from Summers's crisis-era policies
O'Neil remains skeptical and worried despite the appeal of this theory
money_quotes: Larry Summers is being put in the running for the Fed Chair alongside Janet Yellen just so that, when Yellen gets the call, we can all breathe a sigh of relief it didn't go to Summers.
It's a wholly political ploy so the Obama can look like a hero for women everywhere when he chooses Yellen.
We can all conclude that at least Obama's learned this one lesson with regards to dealing with the ongoing financial crisis: Summers isn't the solution.
Depending on my mood I sometimes buy into this theory, but obviously I'm still worried.
key_insight: Political appointments can involve deliberate misdirection, where an unpopular candidate is floated to make the eventual choice seem like a victory.
